rationale: The citing paper explicitly models unobserved/evolving consumer choice sets and aims to identify awareness-driven inclusion effects, while the cited paper’s central contribution is retrieving unobserved consideration sets from household panel choices via a two-stage model—strong conceptual/method overlap. With this close alignment plus an exact DOI match in the reference list (10.1509/jmkr.47.1.63), the citation is plausibly substantive rather than an obvious background-only mention.
